does: This paper presents a comprehensive control roadmap for securing AI agent systems by integrating traditional software safeguards with real-time monitoring mechanisms. The approach combines established security practices—such as access controls, logging, and system isolation—with continuous oversight of agent behavior to detect and prevent deviations from intended operation. The work articulates concrete technical strategies for monitoring agent actions, constraining their capabilities within defined boundaries, and maintaining human oversight throughout deployment. The roadmap addresses the practical challenge of keeping AI systems secure as they become increasingly autonomous and integrated into critical infrastructure, proposing specific architectural patterns and monitoring protocols that can be implemented in real-world systems.
connects: This work builds on decades of security engineering practices in traditional software systems while responding to emerging concerns about AI system alignment and control raised in recent safety literature. It extends prior work on AI containment, interpretability, and monitoring by grounding abstract safety principles in implementable system design. The paper connects to research on verification of autonomous systems, red-teaming methodologies, and formal verification of software, adapting these established techniques to the novel challenges posed by learned models that may exhibit unexpected behaviors. It acknowledges the limitations of static safety guarantees and draws on operational security practices to create dynamic, adaptive oversight mechanisms.
enables: This roadmap enables organizations to deploy increasingly capable AI agents while maintaining meaningful human control and system accountability. By establishing concrete control mechanisms and monitoring practices, it creates a foundation for safe scaling of agent autonomy in high-stakes domains such as finance, healthcare, and critical infrastructure. The work points toward future systems where AI agents operate under continuous verification without requiring prohibitive performance overhead. It opens pathways for developing better monitoring techniques, improving real-time anomaly detection in agent behavior, and creating standards for AI system security that parallel existing software security practices, ultimately supporting the development of trustworthy AI systems that stakeholders can confidently deploy.
